slides.title: 2.2 Practices for Migrating MPLS Networks to SRv6

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: https://notes.ietf.org/notes-ietf-126-srv6ops

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: remote is the audio good?

chat.author: Robert Raszuk
chat.text: Yup ... very good audio

chat.author: Christian Kuhtz
chat.text: please eat the mic :) could stand to be a tad louder in the room.

chat.author: Christian Kuhtz
chat.text: @dhruv thank you!

chat.author: Joel Halpern
chat.text: I presume when he says "across networks" he means across multiple networks within the DT umbrella?

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: yes

chat.author: Joel Halpern
chat.text: Thanks.

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: I am curious what issues/headaches along the migration journey to SRv6. e.g. interop issues

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: thanks Nic/DT great presentation

chat.author: Holger Keller
chat.text: @reshad, we had lot's of interop problems with SR/MPLS, can't be worse this time (now i said it...).  But we will see, when the PoCs are over and we start validation in the  lab. Guess there will be enough issues for an Srv6ops presentation :)

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: Thanks @Holger

chat.author: Daniel Bernier
chat.text: interesting question to understand is how much interop/interwork required vs co-existence and phase-out

chat.author: Boris Khasanov
chat.text: @Dan, depends on how many vendors are in the network, IMO...

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: as a rule of thumb, 2 vendors per network domain is the usual

chat.author: Daniel Bernier
chat.text: @Boris, IMO it is also about the effort of upgrading / migrating devices that might be on the chopping block in the short-mid term.

chat.author: Boris Khasanov
chat.text: yes, surely, especially for  brownfield

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: @Dan B, can you send your question on L3VPN to EVPN to the list plz

chat.author: Daniel Bernier
chat.text: yes I will @Dan V

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: Great presentation Yongqing ! thank you!

chat.author: Yongqing Zhu
chat.text: Thanks Dan. My pleasure.

chat.author: Tom Hill
chat.text: As someone who's very sensitive to being sold to, I didn't find the SONiC presentation to be 'too much marketing'.

chat.author: Weiqiang Cheng
chat.text: one quick question：Ecore's router is pizza box or Chasis based. Could Sonic support chasis based hardware this moment?

chat.author: Donald Sharp
chat.text: as a note FRR releases 3 times a year in Feb/June/Nov.  Sonic is released 2 times a year.  Sonic chooses a FRR release to consolidate on.  Typically this is done via discussion about features needed and bug fixes.  So Sonic is behind to a small extent behind master FRR.  Not a huge problem though from my perspective

chat.author: Weiqiang Cheng
chat.text: @ Ahmed

chat.author: Donald Sharp
chat.text: If you have questions feel free to come and talk to me about this.

chat.author: Donald Sharp
chat.text: also FRR releases are time based not feature based.  If you make the cut-off to the branch pull for the release you are in.

chat.author: Jorge Rabadan
chat.text: @Mike McBride as mentioned last ietf, it would be great if you presented the services aspects of the draft in BESS

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: Thank you @Donald Sharp.

chat.author: Keyur Patel
chat.text: Sending it out as requested by the chais 1: SAI != opensource and 2: would love to see more technical data including scale and other functionalities supported :)

chat.author: Keyur Patel
chat.text: S/chais/Chairs

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: tea runs the world :slight_smile:

chat.author: Christian Kuhtz
chat.text: @weiqiang there is an HLD for chassis and there are implementations on chassis.

chat.author: Christian Kuhtz
chat.text: @weiqiang start here https://github.com/sonic-net/SONiC/wiki/SONiC-Chassis-Subgroup

chat.author: Daniel Bernier
chat.text: @keyur SAI project is an open community effort, ASIC vendors support of  release X or Y of SAI is their prerogative. Hence some ASIC vendors will officially or unofficially support a specific release or might decide to be selective on which of their NPU flavors will support

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: One question to the WG - would they like to to published as RFC or keep as a living document as an adopted I-D

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: this is for draft-ietf-srv6ops-problem-summary

chat.author: Keyur Patel
chat.text: @DanB: am quite aware of SAI being an open community effort. My comments were specific to binary code presence which I believe you know as well. :)

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: @Dhruv draft-ietf-srv6ops-problem-summary as informational RFC

chat.author: Robert Raszuk
chat.text:
Isn't this list where the supported platfroms are listed ? 
https://sonic-net.github.io/SONiC/Supported-Devices-and-Platforms.html

chat.text: @Robert, I guess that list doesn't cover SRv6 specific SAI extensions...

chat.text: @keyur there is open versions of SAI (sonic-vpp, SONIC-P4 now agreed that the might not necessarily follow every release of SAI

chat.author: Ketan Talaulikar
chat.text: @mike and co-authors, I remember seeing time on the BESS agenda this IETF. You could request the chairs to present the migration techniques there. Imho it would be very timely and a good discussion.

chat.author: Ketan Talaulikar
chat.text: I am referring to the migration draft

chat.author: Ketan Talaulikar
chat.text: i. e. +1 to what @Jorge said

chat.author: Dhruv Dhody
chat.text: commenting here - @wataru, for the SFC I-D, it would be good to understand what is SRv6/IPv6 specific as it includes lot more generic information

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: Dear @Wataru, you might have a look at https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-claise-opsawg-ipfix-ordered-ie on addressing imprediments in RFC7011 on multi-layer data plane visibility.
